meta.note: Compact crew stat cards for live show. Not full sheets — just enough for phone screens.

characters.name: Rosario 'The Pink'

flicker.effect: Once/session, vanish entirely — advantage on one group Stealth check, but take 1d4 psychic damage from the effort.

specials: Move through occupied spaces unnoticed
1/long rest: turn an area of milk pink as a distraction (heavily obscured, 10 ft. radius, 1 round)
characters.personality: Communicates only through gestures and a saxophone riff from nowhere.

characters.name: Barrel
specials: Can throw barrels through a 5 ft. line, hitting all creatures in path (DEX DC 15 to dodge)
Surprisingly gentle when not agitated — advantage on Animal Handling
characters.personality: Throws barrels. It's just how he expresses himself.

characters.name: Rosie
specials: Produces 4 shortcakes per long rest — eating one during a rest heals extra 1d4 HP
Presence makes the air smell like strawberries — advantage on Persuasion with friendly NPCs
characters.personality: Maternal, organized, fiercer than she looks. Runs the Shortcake Bakery.

characters.name: Chomp
specials: Advantage on navigation checks in fog, darkness, or obscured conditions
1/day: identify the optimal route through any maze-like environment (auto-success)
characters.personality: Perpetually hungry. Sees the world as pathways, walls, and dots of light. Four ghostly figures follow at a distance.

characters.name: Blueberry
specials: Crew morale never drops below 'content' while Blueberry cooks
Everything touched turns slightly blue — can mark objects or trails
characters.personality: Speaks in third person. 'Blueberry thinks we should go left.' Cheerful and communal.

characters.name: Junior
specials: Can climb any surface without a check at full speed
Advantage on Acrobatics — nimble on rigging, masts, and deck work
characters.personality: Barrel's kid. Nimble where his parent is powerful. Carries fruit everywhere.

characters.name: Goldie
specials: Advantage on Persuasion with unfamiliar cultures and strangers
Can read and decode any written language — automatic success
characters.personality: Anxious, rule-following, constantly calculating survival odds. Hates ships. Complains constantly. Invaluable.

characters.name: The Founder
specials: NOT CREW — trains Allistra (Libby's level-up moment)
Ancestral T-Brawler — built the first fighting arena on IP Isle
characters.personality: Massive, gold-chained, booming voice. 'I pity the fool!' The lore anchor for T-Brawlers.

characters.name: Gizmo
specials: Grant advantage on one ally's roll per short rest (singing buff)
DANGER: If exposed to raw milk or fed after moonrise, spawns 1d4 Moglings (CR 1/2 each) that the party must handle
characters.personality: Small, furry, enormous eyes. Unsettlingly sweet singing voice. Helpful but catastrophically dangerous if mishandled.

characters.name: Glow
specials: Healing Touch: 3/day, heal 1d8+3 HP on touch
Phone Home: ONE-USE distress signal that reaches across the entire Bowl (plot device)
characters.personality: Wrinkly, gentle, barely speaks. Bone-deep longing to 'go home' — but home is beyond the Rim. The saddest person on IP Isle.

characters.name: Specter
specials: Detects sog-corruption within 120 ft. (milk nearby turns green)
1/long rest: trap a small sog-manifestation in containment device
characters.personality: Deadpan, scientific. Treats horror as fascinating. Most knowledgeable about Soggoth's influence.

characters.name: Sarge
specials: 1/encounter: grant one ally an extra bonus action on their next turn
Six tactical modes — can switch focus (leadership, naval, etc.) as a bonus action
characters.personality: Disciplined. Clipped tactical sentences. Has a mini-comic library.

characters.name: Prisma
specials: Project light up to 300 ft. — long-range signaling
1/day: blinding flash — DC 14 CON save or blinded 1 round, 30 ft. cone
characters.personality: Hair and clothes shift through rainbow hues. Cheerful, artistic, powerful.

characters.name: Patches
specials: Advantage on all Strength checks despite small size
On successful grapple, target is restrained until they escape (DC 16)
characters.personality: Round-faced, sweet, arrived fully formed from the Well as if 'adopted' into existence. Deceptively strong.

characters.name: Split
specials: Advantage on Perception — two sets of eyes, two perspectives (watches port and starboard simultaneously)
1/day: split into two half-sized versions for 1 minute (each has half HP, can act independently)
characters.personality: Literal dividing line down the middle — two flavors, two personalities, two opinions about everything.

characters.name: Pixel
specials: Advantage on navigation checks; sees world as zones to clear
1/day: produce random useful item from bottomless satchel (d6: rope, potion, tool, weapon, key, map)
characters.personality: Red-capped adventurer. Speaks in game-logic: 'We need to clear this area before the next zone.'

characters.name: Chef
specials: Long rest meals heal extra 1d6 HP to all who eat
Short rest snacks grant temp HP equal to proficiency bonus (3)
characters.personality: Large, enthusiastic, barely comprehensible accent. Communicates through food. Everything is incredible.

characters.name: Funnies
specials: 1/day: 'read' a situation — advantage on one Investigation or Insight check
BUT: each specific application only works ONCE EVER (you can only read the same comics so many times)
characters.personality: Made of stories — covered in comic-panel markings that shift and change.

characters.name: Axle
specials: Ship repair at advantage — speed modifications specialist
Can jury-rig a speed boost: ship moves double speed for 1 round, 1/day
characters.personality: Speed-obsessed metalworker. Carries die-cast miniature ships in pockets.

characters.name: Doughnut
specials: Always awake before everyone else — never surprised during dawn hours
Morning rations grant advantage on first ability check of the day to all who eat
characters.personality: Round, glazed, early-rising. 'Time to make the doughnuts' is greeting AND philosophy.

characters.name: Shell

flicker.effect: None — one of the most stable Golden Agers.

specials: Reaction: shield adjacent ally with shell — reduce incoming damage by 3 (proficiency bonus)
Unarmed strikes deal 1d8+STR — fights with paddle, chain, or boat hook
characters.personality: Martial artist from the undercity. Obsessed with trying every cuisine. Loyal to a fault once bonded.

characters.name: Cowl

flicker.effect: None — one of the strongest echoes ever.

specials: Darkvision 120 ft. Advantage on Stealth at night.
Smoke pellets: 1/short rest, create heavily obscured area (10 ft. radius)
characters.personality: Masked, caped, gravelly whisper that's slightly ridiculous. Secretly desperate for connection.

characters.name: Radical
specials: 1/short rest: give one ally advantage on any roll ('Most excellent!')
If that roll succeeds, Radical gains inspiration. If it fails, Radical takes 1d4 psychic ('bogus timeline').
characters.personality: Surfer slang, relentlessly optimistic. Carries a phone-booth-shaped cassette holder he treasures.

characters.name: Gem
specials: 5 outfit changes/day — each grants advantage on one social skill (Persuasion, Intimidation, Deception, Performance, or Insight)
Carries a rare genuine gemstone — can be used as a valuable trade good or plot device
characters.personality: Glamorous, five different 'looks,' surprisingly athletic. Arrived with a real diamond.

characters.name: Orbit

flicker.effect: Knows existence is fragile. Alternates between anxious and recklessly brave.

specials: Advantage on repair checks — but on nat 1, the 'improvement' makes things worse in a funny way
Hover-disc: 10 ft. flight for 1 minute, 1/day
characters.personality: Expected a far more advanced world. Deeply disappointed but making the best of it. Has a robot dog that doesn't work properly.

characters.name: Toon

flicker.effect: Slightly less stable than Golden Agers.

specials: Allies within 15 ft. have advantage on saves vs. Frightened (annoyingly upbeat)
Can take the Dash action as a bonus action
characters.personality: Louder, bouncier, more colorful. Determined to prove he's not just a copy of someone better.

characters.name: Specs

flicker.effect: Long echo — stable and fully realized, just chaotic.

specials: Can jury-rig anything: DC 12 Tinker check — success = works perfectly, failure = 1d4 force damage to Specs + comical effect
Audience votes on what Specs builds (interactive moment)
characters.personality: Bespectacled, suspenders. Has broken three things since arriving. 'Did I do that?'

characters.name: Morticia
specials: Never sleeps (doesn't need to). Immune to Frightened condition.
Darkvision 60 ft. Carries a flashlight shaped like a disembodied hand.
characters.personality: Pale, elegant. Finds everything horrifying to be delightful and everything normal to be deeply disturbing.

characters.name: Flex

flicker.effect: Brief existence. Compensates by being INCREDIBLY loud. Secretly terrified of fading.

specials: +2 damage on ALL attacks — massive output
Disadvantage on Stealth ALWAYS. Each attack comes with a wrestling callout.
characters.personality: Massive wrestler with four 'personas' he cycles through. Cannot be subtle about anything.

characters.name: Champ
specials: 1/encounter: grant one ally +1 to hit for the rest of the encounter
Fusion of three athlete archetypes — fights crime for reasons nobody can explain
characters.personality: Athletic prodigy. 'Can win breakfast with anyone.' Multi-sport talent.

characters.name: Hood
characters.source: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves Cereal

flicker.effect: 10% chance per scene of simply not being available. Extremely brief echo.

specials: +7 to hit with ranged attacks — best marksman on the crew
FLICKER: 10% chance per scene of not being available. When he IS there, devastating.
characters.personality: Kevin Costner energy — good at what he does but awkward about being famous. Refused to appear on any official records.

characters.name: Cowl (Dark Variant)

flicker.effect: Brief echo. Coexists uneasily with original Cowl.

specials: Same as Cowl but trades smoke pellets for chocolate smoke (CON DC 12 or distracted 1 round by smell)
Penguin companion: underwater scout, 60 ft. swim speed
characters.personality: Darker, more chocolate-flavored duplicate of 1989 Cowl. Existential crisis about being a copy.

characters.name: Webb

flicker.effect: From the Fading Years. Thinner echo than Golden Age.

specials: Advantage on Initiative, can't be surprised (danger-sense tingles)
Web-line: 30 ft. grapple/swing, 3/day. Can climb any surface at full speed.
characters.personality: Quips constantly. NEVER stands next to Shell. Same base form, different markings — he knows. Shell doesn't.

characters.name: Rex

flicker.effect: Hums with residual sound-magic from audio chips.

specials: Roar: 1/encounter, 30 ft. cone — WIS DC 14 or Frightened 1 round
Carries a treasure map to somewhere that doesn't exist in the Bowl (plot hook)
characters.personality: Huge, thunderous. Smart, patient, hungry. Chose civilization.

characters.name: Reptar

flicker.effect: 25% chance per scene of phasing out temporarily. Existed for SIX WEEKS. Should have faded. Refused.

specials: While present: ALL allies have advantage on saves vs. Frightened, Charmed, or despair effects
All nearby liquid turns green on contact — useful as tracking dye or signal
characters.personality: Fierce tiny green dinosaur. The most beloved underdog on IP Isle. Everyone protects Reptar. Everyone.

characters.name: Bolt
specials: Advantage on Nature/Arcana checks to identify any creature
Three small companion creatures (water, grass, ghost-type) — can scout independently within 200 ft.
characters.personality: Small, electric yellow, red cheeks. Immediately began cataloging every species on IP Isle. 'Gotta catch 'em all' as philosophy.

characters.name: Cipher
specials: Auto-succeeds on decoding checks — reads any ancient text, Thieves' Cant, coded messages
Submerged in milk: markings reveal hidden messages (plot device)
characters.personality: Archaeologist/linguist. 'Perhaps the most intellectually ambitious' IP Islander. Obsessed with IP Isle's origins.

characters.name: Xara

flicker.effect: Never sold in normal markets — only specialty shops. Strangest distribution of any IP Islander.

specials: Three fighting stances — switch as bonus action: Offensive (+2 damage), Defensive (+2 AC), Balanced
Three forms (warrior, companion, villain variant) — different energy each form
characters.personality: Warrior with three forms. Was NEVER sold in normal markets. The strangest distribution of any IP Islander.

characters.name: Homer

flicker.effect: Ongoing echo — stable. Knows he's fictional. Doesn't care.

specials: Immune to Poison — can safely consume ANY substance to identify it (taste-tester for unknown materials)
Defictionalized — a story within a story that became real. Homer knows he's fictional. It doesn't bother him.
characters.personality: Large, lazy, donut-obsessed. Actually terrible at cooking. Just wants donuts.

characters.name: Odyssey (Pixel 2.0)
specials: All of Pixel's abilities (advantage on navigation, random item satchel)
Scan: 1/day — touch an object to reveal hidden magical properties, curses, or secrets. Amiibo box hums near concealed magic.
characters.personality: Shinier, more polished Pixel. Same red cap, same game-logic. Some islanders prefer the original.

characters.name: Helm
specials: Advantage on Survival to track any creature. AC 18 (Beskar-equivalent armor).
Bean (tiny green infant): Mage Hand at will. 1/day Force Push — STR DC 14 or pushed 15 ft.
characters.personality: Never removes the helmet. Never. This is the Way. Short, definitive sentences.

characters.name: Blockhead
specials: Repair ship with ANY raw material (halves repair time). Breaks through barriers others can't.
1/day: construct a 10 ft. wall of blocks (20 HP, lasts 1 hour)
characters.personality: Cubic, pixelated. Sees world in blocks. Mortal fear of a green hissing exploding creature (Creeper roams the outskirts).

characters.name: Trickster

flicker.effect: Exists in a quantum state. You can never find Trickster when you look. Appears only when they choose to.

specials: At will: Disguise Self, Misty Step, Minor Illusion
CATCH: Once per session, Trickster does something self-serving that complicates things (DM choice), then immediately fixes it.
characters.personality: Most LIMITED IP Islander ever. Sold out in seconds. Mischievous, brilliant, untrustworthy in the best way.

characters.name: Specter Jr.

flicker.effect: Revival of the 1985 echo — younger, updated.

specials: Detects sog-corruption within 90 ft. (shorter range than original Specter)
1/long rest: trap a small sog-manifestation in containment device
characters.personality: Younger Specter. Slightly less experienced but same energy. Redundant if original Specter is recruited.

characters.name: Dunk
characters.source: Space Jam: A New Legacy Cereal

flicker.effect: Modern echo — polished but not ancient.

specials: Inspiring presence: allies within 30 ft. get +1 to attack rolls
Arrived with a cartoon rabbit companion — provides comic relief and minor scouting
characters.personality: Athletic, competitive, LeBron-energy. Massive presence. Basketball player who also fights.

characters.name: Dash
specials: Speed 60 ft. Can run across liquid surfaces for up to 1 minute.
Carries 2d20 gold rings worth 1 alpie each — arrived wealthy
characters.personality: Blue, impossibly fast. Impatient with anything slow. Taps foot while waiting. Collects gold rings obsessively.

characters.name: Toast

flicker.effect: Defictionalized — a story within a story that became real.

specials: Immune to fire damage (already toasted). Flight 30 ft.
1/day: propel through a solid barrier up to 6 inches thick, leaving a toast-shaped hole
characters.personality: Muscular figure made of literal toast, wearing a cape. Knows he shouldn't exist. Considers this hilarious.

characters.name: Waffle

flicker.effect: Brand new. Carries D20 dice. Knows things about the world the players come from. Deeply unsettling.

specials: 1/session: audience rolls a physical die that affects the game outcome (THE meta-moment)
Upside Down window: 1/day, reveals one hidden truth — but something notices
characters.personality: Newest IP Islander. Maple waffle markings, marshmallow flashlights, and actual D20 dice. Almost touches the real world.

characters.name: Wednesday

attack.name: Cold Stare
attack.damage: WIS DC 15 or Frightened 1 round + answer one question truthfully

specials: Auto-detects lies — always knows when someone is lying
Advantage on Insight checks always. Cold Stare: 1/encounter, WIS DC 15 or Frightened + answer truthfully.
characters.personality: Deadpan, all black, never smiles. Considers IP Isle 'tediously cheerful.' Secretly cares deeply.
